title: Optimized Binary Search with Incorrect Handling of Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown: Write an optimized binary search function for finding an element in a sorted array. The function should return the index of the element if found, or -1 if the element is not present. However, the function has a logical bug and fails to handle duplicate elements correctly. Fix the bug to ensure the function works correctly for arrays with duplicate elements.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sorted array of integers and a target element to be searched.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the index of the target element if found, or -1 if the element is not present.
statement.notes: The function should use binary search to achieve an average time complexity of O(log n).

constraints.description: The number of elements in the array
The target element to be searched

examples.explanation: The target element 3 is present at index 2 in the array.
The target element 6 is not present in the array.
The target element 2 is present at index 1 in the array.

hints.text: Review the binary search algorithm and understand how it works.
Identify the logical bug in the given code and fix it to handle duplicate elements correctly.
Use the properties of binary search to optimize the function and achieve an average time complexity of O(log n).

editorial.title: Optimized Binary Search with Duplicate Elements
editorial.intuition: Binary search is an efficient algorithm for finding an element in a sorted array. However, when dealing with duplicate elements, the function needs to be modified to handle them correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to iterate through the array and check each element, but this would result in a time complexity of O(n), which is inefficient for large arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use binary search and handle duplicate elements correctly. This can be achieved by modifying the function to return the index of the first occurrence of the target element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a modified binary search function that handles duplicate elements correctly. This can be achieved by maintaining a pointer to the first occurrence of the target element and returning its index.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness for the modified binary search function involves showing that it returns the correct index for the target element in the presence of duplicate elements.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the modified binary search function with an example input can help verify its correctness and performance.
commonMistakes: Failing to handle duplicate elements correctly

functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, nums, target: int) -> int:
